reason_for_decision: The article clears the pass bar by focusing on integration as a workflow problem involving role separation, timing, coordinate alignment, and operator closure.
strengths: The article explains that shared networking is not the same as real integration, which is the right architectural starting point.
Its treatment of time synchronization, coordinate registration, and confidence logic is especially strong.
The operator-closure and degraded-mode sections make the guidance practical rather than theoretical.
summary_assessment: This article passes comfortably. It is one of the stronger system-design pieces in the queue because it treats fusion as an operational chain instead of a generic feature.
